behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often a live model/index mismatch (e.g., embedding model version outputs different dimensions than index schema). A stubbed embedding would hide that. This behavior checks actual model output shape, so LLM-DEP/ML-DEP applies. Rejected `Workflow` because the behavior is a single model-output compatibility check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model with a fixed query, fetch the live index dimension from the vector DB schema, and assert exact dimension equality; fail on any mismatch.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is at real vector-search infrastructure behavior (DB metric config, score direction, ANN return ordering, transport/adapter handling). In-process mocks can miss these. Integration is insufficient if it does not exercise real DB behavior. Rejected `Agentic` because correctness here is numeric ordering of similarity scores, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vector DB instance/index, insert controlled vectors, run similarity query through the API search path, and assert returned scores are monotonic non-increasing and IDs match expected cosine order.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly about relevance/ranking quality from a real LLM decision; deterministic fixtures would always pass and cannot validate reranking behavior. Rejected `Workflow` because only one agentic decision (rerank) is being validated, not multiple model decision stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run real retrieval + real reranker on curated nuanced queries where lexical similarity conflicts with contextual intent; assert reranked order differs from raw top-K in expected direction for at least one case.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Whether a query is truly 'no close match' depends on real embedding-space semantics and relevance behavior; mocks can mask false positives. This is output relevance correctness, so content-quality priority pushes to Agentic. Rejected `Workflow` because this behavior can be validated at the single model-driven matching decision boundary (no required multi-agentic chain assertion).
behaviors.test_description: Use a real embedding model with an intentionally out-of-domain query set against a populated production-like index, execute search path, and assert no results are returned above the configured relevance threshold.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: The metric covers the full pipeline with multiple real agentic steps in sequence (embedding call + retrieval + LLM rerank) plus infrastructure effects. Lower-level tests with stubs cannot expose true median latency behavior. Rejected `System` because the behavior text is explicitly end-to-end across chained agentic stages, matching Workflow definition.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like load test through the real API path with real embedding and reranking services enabled, collect end-to-end latency distribution, and assert p50 < 2.0s over a representative query corpus.
